lines.text: Trimester project. One model, one scientific principle, one week. A district judge will be here for presentations, so — no, you may not just glue a volcano together and call it thermodynamics.
Please don't put me with the kid who narrates his own gym class.
Groups are on the board. Castillo — Okafor, Nassar.
lines.action: taps pen twice against desk

lines.text: That's us. Nice.
lines.action: leans back, already scanning the room like he's picked his starting lineup

lines.text: Nice like 'good group' nice, or nice like you already have a plan I should be worried about?
Little bit of both.
Hi.
lines.action: brief eye contact, then back to her notebook

lines.text: Hey. I'm Wren. We've got the same lunch block, right?
Yeah. You sit by the window.
She noticed that. Okay. Filing that under 'people who actually pay attention.'
Okay, so, good news.
lines.action: pulls out a flash drive, spins it once on the table

lines.text: That's usually how it starts right before the bad news.
My brother did basically this exact project two years ago. Same category, same board, everything. Got an A.
We could just... update the numbers a little. Change some labels. Nobody's gonna check.
lines.action: cracks his knuckles
goes very still, pen hovering over her sketchbook, says nothing

lines.text: Cool. Cool cool cool. Everyone's just gonna look at me now, huh.
It's not cheating, it's efficiency. There's a difference — look it up.
Fine. Fine! We'll do it the boring way first, see how far that gets us.
That's all I'm asking. Boring and ours.
They spread out three textbooks and a laptop. It's slower. Nobody says that out loud.
I mean — it's just an idea. We don't have to do it that way. But what if we used water pressure instead of air. It's cheaper to build and it's kind of a cooler visual.
lines.action: already looking back down at her sketchbook before she finishes talking

lines.text: Wait, say that again. That's actually good.
...Okay.
lines.action: small, startled smile, gone almost instantly

lines.text: See? Look at that. Half our board's basically done.
lines.action: scrolling fast, energized

lines.text: Yeah. That is... a lot faster.
It's fine.
lines.action: staring at the screen, tapping her pen once against her sketchbook and stopping herself
lines.emotion: flat, in the tone that means it's not fine

lines.text: We're just using it as a starting point. We'll change stuff.
We haven't changed anything yet. That's kind of the point of a starting point, I guess.
I mean, it's probably not a big deal, but — what if we simplified the design? Fewer moving parts, but it'd actually be more interesting to watch. Judges like watching things.
That'll take too long. We're doing it my way.
lines.action: already moving on, not even looking up

lines.text: ...Okay.
lines.action: shrinks back into her chair, closes her sketchbook halfway

lines.text: That's the second time. She's not even arguing anymore, she's just — bracing.
Actually, I think Priya's idea's better. The judge is gonna sit through like fifteen baking-soda volcanoes today. Something that actually moves is gonna stand out.
...Wait, since when are you two a package deal?
Since it's a good idea, Dez.
...It really would be more interesting.
lines.action: sits up straighter than she has all week

lines.text: ...Fine. Fine, we'll try it. If it flops it's on both of you.

lines.action: opens her mouth, then just starts writing notes instead

lines.text: Cool, moving on.
lines.emotion: already onto the next thing, oblivious

lines.action: goes quiet for the rest of the session — not shy-quiet, retreating-quiet

lines.text: Nobody else even noticed. That's the worst part.
Hey — Priya, wait up.
lines.action: catches up outside the library doors

lines.text: ...Hey.
Your idea today. The simpler design. It was actually the better one. I should've said that in there.

lines.action: shoulders loosen slightly, but her eyes flick away fast

lines.text: It's fine. He does that.
Doesn't mean it should keep being fine.
...Thanks, Wren.
lines.action: brief, real eye contact, then looks down again

lines.text: Okay. Trial two's a disaster, trial four's missing numbers entirely. We are running out of clock, people.
lines.action: on video call, rubbing his face

lines.text: Yeah, I noticed the clock. It's the only thing louder than you right now.
lines.emotion: trying for a joke, not quite landing

lines.text: We could smooth it. Adjust the numbers to match what we expected to find anyway. It's basically the same thing, just cleaner.

lines.action: on screen, very still, camera light reflecting in her glasses

lines.text: It's due tomorrow morning, Wren. We don't have a lot of options left on the table.
Okay, let's just run trial two again. Live. Right now, on camera, both of you watch me not mess it up.
lines.emotion: determined, a little manic from tiredness

lines.text: It's 9:40, Wren.
It's 9:40 and I have a working sink. Let's go.
Forty minutes later, three tired kids are staring at a spreadsheet like it personally wronged them.
Wait. Go back. Your ratio's flipped in row six — that's the whole problem.
lines.action: leaning into her camera, suddenly sharp and awake

lines.text: ...No way. NO way. Say that again, I need to hear it twice.
She just saved this whole project at 10 PM and you want a rerun?
lines.action: laughing

lines.text: I mean — I'm right, so.
lines.emotion: dry, almost smug, dropping the hedge completely

lines.text: ...Fine. Just this once. We adjust it to what it should've been.
See, that's all I'm saying. In and out, ten minutes.

lines.action: types the new numbers without looking at either of them

lines.text: The spreadsheet fills in clean, symmetrical, perfect. It takes eight minutes.
...Okay. That's it. Night.
Night.
lines.action: the call ends and the room feels too quiet

lines.text: These results are unusually clean.
lines.action: taps pen twice against the data table

lines.text: Walk me through how you got this measurement.

lines.action: goes very quiet, eyes flicking toward Wren
still, pen tapping once against her sketchbook and stopping

lines.text: Three sets of eyes. Great. Love this. Love it here.
The timeline got really tight last night, and some of trial two's data had to be reworked. It's not... totally raw. I should've flagged it sooner.
lines.emotion: steady, slower than her usual fast-nervous talk

lines.text: I'm not thrilled. I'm also not going to pretend I didn't hear you say that.
lines.action: nods slowly, writes something on her clipboard

lines.text: You'll present with a note on the data table. That's fair to the work you actually did.
...Yeah. That's — yeah. That's fair.

lines.action: exhales, shoulders dropping about an inch

lines.text: I think that part might've been an error on Priya's end? She had trial two.
lines.emotion: too quick, regretting it as it leaves her mouth

lines.action: face falls, completely still, doesn't correct it in front of everyone
very quiet, visibly relieved it landed somewhere else, doesn't meet anyone's eyes

lines.text: Nassar? Anything to add?
...No. It's fine.
lines.action: the exact tone that means it is not fine

lines.text: There was some confusion in one of the trials — we split up sections and it slipped through between us. We fixed what we could catch in time.
Mm. Alright.
lines.action: taps her pen once, decides not to push further
quiet, avoiding eye contact with both of them

lines.text: Nobody got humiliated. Nobody got the truth either. I don't know why that feels worse.
The bike racks. Priya checks her phone. Dez is a few yards off, fighting his lock like it owes him money.
My mom's probably two minutes out.
Okay. Last chance to say literally anything real today.
Hey — I should've said something earlier today. In front of everyone. I'm sorry I didn't.

lines.action: blinks, caught off guard

lines.text: New rule, next project: no faking results, no talking over each other. I'll even make you sign it.
...You'd actually make a poster for that, wouldn't you.
lines.action: laughs, surprised by her own laugh
lines.emotion: warm, disbelieving in a good way

lines.text: Bullet points. Maybe a font.
...Okay. Deal.
lines.action: looks like she actually believes it, for the first time all week

lines.text: So, uh. You doing anything this weekend?
Not really. Probably drawing.
A car pulls up. Priya grabs her bag.
See you Monday.
lines.action: quick wave, already turning away

lines.text: Yeah. See you.
lines.action: watches the car pull out

lines.text: That was easier. It didn't feel easier.
Priya's mom pulls up. She climbs in with a quick wave, and it's just Wren and Dez, and the world's most stubborn bike lock.
Dez. Today — the thing with Priya's idea, and then throwing her under the bus with Alvarez. That wasn't okay.
I didn't throw her under anything, I just—
lines.action: stops, doesn't finish, still fighting the lock

lines.text: You kind of did.

lines.action: finally stops messing with the lock, doesn't look up

lines.text: ...Yeah, okay. That wasn't — I shouldn't have talked to her like that. Whatever. It's not a big deal.
lines.emotion: quiet, deflecting even as he says it

lines.text: It kind of is. But — thanks for saying it.

options.text: "That's basically cheating. Let's do our own."
"I mean... it could save us time. Let's just look."

a.text: It's not cheating, it's— okay, fine, whatever, we'll do it your way.

b.text: See, this is why we work.

b.action: says nothing, watches the screen fill up with numbers that aren't theirs

options.text: Back Priya's idea out loud, right now.
Stay quiet and let Dez keep leading.
Say nothing now, but check in with Priya after.

a.emotion: quiet, but standing a little taller

c.action: makes a note to catch Priya after — this time, actually do it

options.text: "Let's just redo it right, even if it takes longer."
"Fine — let's just clean it up and submit."

a.text: It's almost ten, Wren, but— okay. Okay, let's go.

b.text: Now you're talking. Let's just get this done.

options.text: Admit the timeline was tight and be honest about what happened.
Imply it was Priya's part of the work that went wrong.
Give a vague, half-true answer that protects everyone a little.

a.text: Nobody in this room is in trouble for telling me the truth late. They're in trouble for telling me a story instead.

b.action: says nothing, doesn't correct it, just goes still

c.text: Mm. Alright. We'll talk after class.
c.emotion: not fully satisfied, filing it away

options.text: Apologize to Priya and suggest real ground rules going forward.
Let it go and talk about something else.
Wait for Priya to leave, then tell Dez directly the pressure wasn't okay.

b.action: quick wave, gone before Wren can change her mind

c.text: ...Yeah, okay. That wasn't — I shouldn't have talked to her like that. Whatever. It's not a big deal.

lines.text: We didn't fake anything. It cost us a little on the score. It bought us this instead.
For what it's worth — I noticed. The whole 'redo it right at ten PM' thing. That mattered.
We got a clean score off numbers that weren't really ours. This apology's a start. It's not the whole thing.
...Okay. Deal. Just — actually mean it next time the shortcut's sitting right there.
lines.emotion: hopeful, but with an edge of caution

lines.text: You've actually had my back all week. This isn't even the first time.
Turns out it's easier the second time. Who knew.
Not every call I made this week was the right one. But I made this one.
...Okay. Deal. We'll see how it goes.
lines.emotion: cautiously optimistic, not fully sold yet

lines.text: I told Alvarez the truth about the data. I still couldn't manage five honest minutes with Priya.
See you Monday.
lines.action: quick wave, gone before the silence could turn into anything

lines.text: We faked the data, I let the blame slide sideways, and now I can't even bring myself to say sorry out loud. Great week, Wren.
See you Monday.
lines.action: the wave is smaller than usual, like she's already decided not to expect anything

lines.text: This is the third time this week I've had the chance to say something and didn't.
See you Monday.
lines.action: doesn't quite meet Wren's eyes before turning away

lines.text: Some days I say the thing. Today wasn't one of them.
See you Monday.
lines.action: quick wave, and the car pulls away before Wren can decide if that was okay

lines.text: You know I was straight with Alvarez about the data. I'm being straight with you too.
...Yeah. I noticed. Doesn't make this less annoying to hear, but — yeah.
I went along with the smoothed numbers. I'm not proud of that. But what you did to Priya today — that's still on you to fix.
lines.emotion: honest about her own part, still firm

lines.text: ...We both went along with it, Wren.
lines.action: quiet, not quite defensive, more tired

lines.text: Yeah. We did.
lines.emotion: quiet, no argument left in her

lines.text: This isn't new, Dez. You do this every time she talks. Today it just got seen.
...You've been keeping count, huh.
Somebody had to.
I didn't always say something this week. I'm saying it now.
...Yeah, okay. That wasn't — I shouldn't have talked to her like that. Whatever. It's not a big deal.

voiceNotes: Wren's fast, deflecting humor ('cool cool cool') shows up most under pressure (scene-2, scene-8) and drops into slower, plainer sentences the moment she actually commits to something true (scene-9a, scene-11c) — the slowing down IS the tell that she means it. Dez's bravado is built on sports/efficiency language ('efficiency,' 'clean,' 'in and out') that thins out into short, quiet fragments the moment he's actually caught (scene-8, scene-11c) — he never gets loud when it's real, he gets small. Priya's hedges ('I mean, it's probably not a big deal') are a pressure gauge — count how many times she hedges in a scene to know how safe she feels; she drops them entirely in 7a and 11a as trust builds, and tightens back into silence/'it's fine' when let down (5b, 9b). Ms. Alvarez never accuses — she asks and waits, which is scarier than yelling; her stillness is the punishment, not her volume.

alternatives: Some of the data got reworked last night because we ran out of time. That part's on me — I should've raised it earlier.
Not all of trial two is original. We were up against the clock and I made a call I should've flagged sooner.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Keeps Wren's honesty concrete and self-owned without over-explaining; either works depending on desired formality.

alternatives: I actually think Priya's design is the stronger pitch here.
Can we at least hear her out before we lock it in your way?
alternativeLines.reasoning: Slightly softer entry points if a more hesitant-Wren tone is preferred for this branch.
